# Provider Support Matrix

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-support-matrix.py; do not edit. -->

This table is generated from [`support-matrix.toml`](../support-matrix.toml).
Capability claims remain subject to the consistency and platform restrictions
documented by each provider.

| Provider | ID | Implemented | Current maturity | v1 target | Platforms | Capabilities | Optional capabilities | Mechanism |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Fake Read-Only Provider | `fake-read-only` | Yes | supported | internal | linux, windows, macos | memory_read | None | testkit in-memory sparse map |
| Raw Memory Dump | `raw-dump` | Yes | supported | supported | linux, windows, macos | memory_read | None | immutable flat physical-memory artifact |
| QEMU QMP | `qemu-qmp` | Yes | preview | supported | linux, windows, macos | memory_read, register_read, control, acquisition, events | register_write (with GDB endpoint) | QMP query-status/stop/cont/dump-guest-memory and asynchronous events + HMP xp/info registers/pmemsave |
| Manifest Snapshot Provider | `snapshot-manifest` | Yes | supported | supported | linux, windows, macos | memory_read | None | versioned multi-file manifest normalized to immutable physical ranges |
| Xen | `xen` | Yes | preview | preview | linux | control, acquisition | memory_read/memory_write (libxenctrl); register_read (xenctx or CPU transport); register_write (writable CPU transport); events (vm_event transport) | dynamically loaded libxenctrl foreign-page mappings + xenctx vCPU contexts + xl control/acquisition |
| VirtualBox | `virtualbox` | Yes | experimental | experimental | linux, windows, macos | memory_read, register_read, control, acquisition | direct memory reads through a Main API/XPCOM memory transport; register_write on vendor builds implementing IMachineDebugger::setRegister | VBoxManage showvminfo/controlvm/debugvm registers plus snapshot-backed dumpvmcore memory reads |
| VirtualBox VM Core | `virtualbox-core` | Yes | supported | supported | linux, windows, macos | memory_read | None | dumpvmcore ELF PT_LOAD physical-memory normalization |
| Firecracker | `firecracker` | Yes | experimental | experimental | linux | memory_read | None | versioned Firecracker memory snapshot manifest |
| Cloud Hypervisor | `cloud-hypervisor` | Yes | experimental | experimental | linux | memory_read | None | versioned Cloud Hypervisor memory snapshot manifest |
| Hyper-V | `hyperv` | Yes | experimental | experimental | windows | memory_read | None | versioned saved-state memory manifest |
| Hyper-V Converted Core | `hyperv-core` | Yes | supported | supported | linux, windows, macos | memory_read | None | user-supplied LiveKd/VM Saved State conversion auto-detected as ELF or Windows KDMP |
| VMware | `vmware` | Yes | experimental | experimental | linux, windows, macos | memory_read | None | flat .vmem physical-memory artifact |
| VMware Converted Core | `vmware-core` | Yes | supported | supported | linux, windows, macos | memory_read | None | user-supplied vmss2core output auto-detected as ELF or Windows KDMP |
| bhyve | `bhyve` | Yes | compile-only | experimental | freebsd | memory_read | None | versioned saved-state memory manifest |
| bhyve Converted Core | `bhyve-core` | Yes | preview | experimental | linux, windows, macos, freebsd | memory_read | None | user-supplied bhyve conversion auto-detected as ELF or Windows KDMP |

Current maturity describes shipped evidence today; v1 target is the maximum tier intended for the 1.0 release.
Both labels describe this implementation, not the upstream hypervisor.
